file.content:
+
{busn_garrison}
authenticates using this key only.
First-time admin trust is established by
{busn_caparison}
per platform: Windows uses
`PreferredAuthentications=publickey,password`
on its phase-1 admin SSH session so that on a fresh node ssh prompts
on /dev/tty for the admin password once; Linux/Mac wrap the
operator-manual `ssh-copy-id` flow. After caparison, all subsequent
admin authentication is by key alone.

[[burp_workload_key_file]]
//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_path
{burp_workload_key_file}::
Path to the operator-managed SSH private key the workload account uses
to authenticate inbound (from the operator's station) and outbound
(to GitHub or other services).
Garrison copies this private key onto the remote workload account at a
shell-letter-specific path hardcoded in
{bujb_jurisdiction};
the matching public key is derived via `ssh-keygen -y` for
authorized_keys placement on both sides of the trip.
+
The workload pubkey must be pre-registered with any outbound service
(e.g., GitHub deploy keys) out-of-band; garrison cannot validate that
registration.

=== BURD Regime

[[burd_prefix]]
//axl_voices axrg_prefix
{burd_prefix}::
Bash Utility Dispatch Runtime

[[burd_regime]]
//axvr_regime axf_bash axrd_constructed axrd_singleton
{burd_regime}::
Ephemeral runtime state assembled by bud_dispatch.sh during tabtarget execution.
Unlike file-sourced regimes, BURD variables are constructed inline — no assignment file exists.
The dispatch main function IS the kindle: zbud_setup sources configuration and derives computed state, zbud_process_args parses the tabtarget into tokens.

==== Input Variables

[[burd_group_input]]
//axl_voices axhrgb_group
{burd_group_input}::
Variables set by the caller before dispatch invocation.

[[burd_regime_file]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path
{burd_regime_file}::
Path to the BURC regime assignment file to source during setup.

[[burd_no_log]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string axd_optional
{burd_no_log}::
When non-empty, suppresses all log file creation and output.

[[burd_interactive]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string axd_optional
{burd_interactive}::
When non-empty, enables interactive mode with uncurated tee logging.

[[burd_coordinator_script]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path axd_required
{burd_coordinator_script}::
Path to the coordinator script (workbench/testbench) that handles the dispatched command.

[[burd_launcher]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path axd_required
{burd_launcher}::
Path to the launcher bootstrap script, set by the tabtarget before exec.

[[burd_station_file]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path axd_required
{burd_station_file}::
Absolute path to the developer's station regime assignment file.
Set by the launcher from BURC_STATION_FILE.

[[burd_term_cols]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_integer
{burd_term_cols}::
Terminal column width at dispatch time.
Detected from stty if available, defaults to 80.

==== Computed Variables

[[burd_group_computed]]
//axl_voices axhrgb_group
{burd_group_computed}::
Variables derived during dispatch setup.

[[burd_now_stamp]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string
{burd_now_stamp}::
Unique timestamp combining date, time, PID, and random component.

[[burd_now_epoch]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_integer
{burd_now_epoch}::
UTC epoch seconds captured from same `date` invocation as {burd_now_stamp}.

[[burd_temp_dir]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path
{burd_temp_dir}::
Ephemeral directory for this dispatch invocation.

[[burd_output_dir]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path
{burd_output_dir}::
Fixed output directory cleared and recreated on each dispatch.

[[burd_transcript]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path
{burd_transcript}::
Path to the transcript file within the temporary directory.

[[burd_git_context]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string

1480	{burx_began_at}.
1481	Present only when

1484	
1485	include::BUSD-DispatchRuntime.adoc[]
1486	
1487	== Remote Node Access
1488	
1489	BUK reaches remote nodes under the
1490	{bus_jurisdiction}
1491	umbrella: a single station user establishes admin SSH trust on the
1492	node, provisions one workload account, and dispatches commands to it.
1493	
1494	[[bus_jurisdiction]]
1495	{bus_jurisdiction}::
1496	The BUK feature-area for reaching remote nodes deterministically.
1497	Three verbs cover the privileged-side ceremonies:
1498	{busn_caparison}
1499	establishes admin host posture per platform (SSH key trust,
1500	`sshd_config` hardening on Windows OpenSSH, host-availability
1501	state); {busn_invigilate} verifies that posture read-only as a
1502	precondition for {busn_garrison} and as a post-completion check for
1503	{busn_caparison}; {busn_garrison} provisions and refreshes a workload
1504	account from an existing admin SSH foothold. The workload-side
1505	tabtargets
1506	({bust_knock}, {bust_command_file}, {bust_interactive_session})
1507	dispatch work to that account.
1508	+
1509	{bus_jurisdiction}
1510	is logical only — there is no AXLA-typed entity behind the umbrella.
1511	Its implementation seat is
1512	{bujb_jurisdiction}.
1513	
1514	[[bujb_jurisdiction]]
1515	{bujb_jurisdiction}::
1516	The BCG-compliant module
1517	(`Tools/buk/bujb_jurisdiction.sh` plus its `bujb_cli.sh` companion)
1518	that houses
1519	{busn_caparison},
1520	{busn_invigilate},
1521	and
1522	{busn_garrison}
1523	implementations and hardcodes the
1524	{bus_jurisdiction}
1525	contracts not expressed in regime data:
1526	the three shell-letter→`command=` directive mappings
1527	(`b` = native bash for Linux/Mac;
1528	`c` = Cygwin;
1529	`w` = WSL)
1530	used by garrison on the workload account,
1531	the workload private-key destination paths on the remote per shell-letter,
1532	the canonical WSL distribution name (`rbtww-main`),
1533	and the Windows OpenSSH `sshd_config` hardening directive set used by
1534	caparison-windows.
1535	+
1536	SSH transport mechanics live in code;
1537	{burp_regime}
1538	carries no shell selection.
1539	
1540	[[bujb_workload_user]]
1541	//axvr_constant axf_bash
1542	{bujb_workload_user}::
1543	Project-wide constant name for the unprivileged workload OS user that
1544	{busn_garrison}
1545	provisions on every node — single source of truth, one workload user
1546	per node, never operator-tunable.
1547	Defined as a tinder constant in
1548	{bujb_jurisdiction}
1549	so it resolves at module-source time and is available to every
1550	jurisdiction-domain function without enrollment.
1551	On Windows nodes the same name covers both namespaces of the mirrored
1552	identity (the Windows local user that
1553	{busn_garrison} (`w`) creates via `net.exe user /add` and the Linux
1554	user inside `rbtww-main` it creates via `useradd`).
1555	
1556	=== Privileges
1557	
1558	A
1559	{burn_regime}
1560	instance lives under `.buk/rbmn_nodes/<{burn_investiture}>/` and is shared
1561	across station users (git-transported); it carries node shape only and
1562	no key material.
1563	A
1564	{burp_regime}
1565	instance lives under `.buk/rbmu_users/<{burs_user}>/<{burp_investiture}>/`;
1566	the
1567	{burs_user}
1568	owns its credential subtree exclusively, and operations write only
1569	under the current station user's tree.
1570	
1571	{busn_garrison}
1572	authenticates as
1573	{burp_privileged_user}
1574	using
1575	{burp_privileged_key_file}
1576	by key alone; admin trust and host posture are established
1577	beforehand by
1578	{busn_caparison}
1579	per platform (Windows uses a one-time password fallback on its own
1580	phase-1 admin SSH session for first-run trust; Linux/Mac wrap
1581	ssh-copy-id orchestration).
1582	Workload account is unprivileged, ssh-only, and named by
1583	{bujb_workload_user}
1584	(project-wide convention, single source of truth).
1585	
1586	=== Garrison-Destructive Model
1587	
1588	Every
1589	{busn_garrison}
1590	invocation wipes prior workload state on the node regardless of which
1591	{burp_investiture}
1592	or station user runs it.
1593	There is exactly one workload user per node, ever; node runtime state
1594	belongs to whoever last garrisoned it.
1595	Garrison subsumes the workload-account lifecycle: there is no separate
1596	verb to materialize, remove, or enumerate workload accounts — every
1597	garrison rebuilds from a clean slate.
1598	+
1599	Admin-side host posture is out of garrison's scope.
1600	{busn_caparison}
1601	owns it per platform: Windows OpenSSH `sshd_config` hardening, WSL
1602	distribution staging, sleep/hibernate disable, and Tailscale service
1603	auto-start on Windows; Remote Login + pmset + Tailscale on macOS;
1604	ssh-copy-id orchestration + sleep target masking + tailscaled on
1605	Linux.
1606	{busn_invigilate}
1607	verifies the posture as garrison's precondition.
1608	
1609	=== Tabtarget Catalog
1610	
1611	Twenty-three tabtargets cover regime configuration, caparison,
1612	invigilate, privileged SSH, garrison ceremony, workload dispatch,
1613	and per-platform handbooks.
1614	PowerShell garrison and dispatch are deferred and not enumerated below.
1615	
1616	==== Regime Configuration
1617	
1618	[[bust_node_regime_list]]
1619	//axvo_tabtarget

1621	`buw-rnl` —
1622	List all {burn_regime} profiles under `.buk/rbmn_nodes/`.
1623	
1624	[[bust_node_regime_render]]
1625	//axvo_tabtarget

1627	`buw-rnr` —
1628	Render a single {burn_regime} profile
1629	({burn_investiture} passed as positional argument).
1630

1634	`buw-rnv` —
1635	Validate a single {burn_regime} profile
1636	({burn_investiture} passed as positional argument).
1637	
1638	[[bust_privilege_regime_list]]
1639	//axvo_tabtarget
1640	{bust_privilege_regime_list}::
1641	`buw-rpl` —
1642	List all {burp_regime} profiles for the current {burs_user}.
1643	
1644	[[bust_privilege_regime_render]]
1645	//axvo_tabtarget
1646	{bust_privilege_regime_render}::
1647	`buw-rpr` —
1648	Render a single {burp_regime} profile
1649	({burp_investiture} passed as positional argument).
1650	
1651	[[bust_privilege_regime_validate]]
1652	//axvo_tabtarget
1653	{bust_privilege_regime_validate}::
1654	`buw-rpv` —
1655	Validate a single {burp_regime} profile
1656	({burp_investiture} passed as positional argument).
1657	
1658	==== Caparison (per platform)
1659	
1660	[[bust_caparison_windows]]
1661	//axvo_tabtarget
1662	{bust_caparison_windows}::
1663	`buw-jpCW` —
1664	{busn_caparison} ceremony on a Windows OpenSSH node: SSH-trust
1665	hardening, WSL distribution staging, powercfg sleep/hibernate
1666	disable, and Tailscale service auto-start.
1667	Capital `C` marks the verb (Caparison); capital `W` marks the
1668	platform (Windows).
1669	
1670	[[bust_caparison_macos]]
1671	//axvo_tabtarget
1672	{bust_caparison_macos}::
1673	`buw-jpCM` —
1674	{busn_caparison} ceremony on a macOS node: Remote Login enable,
1675	ssh-copy-id orchestration, pmset power configuration, Tailscale
1676	service auto-start.
1677	
1678	[[bust_caparison_linux]]
1679	//axvo_tabtarget
1680	{bust_caparison_linux}::
1681	`buw-jpCL` —
1682	{busn_caparison} ceremony on a Linux node: ssh-copy-id orchestration,
1683	systemctl sleep target masking, tailscaled enable.
1684	
1685	==== Invigilate (per platform)
1686	
1687	[[bust_invigilate_windows]]
1688	//axvo_tabtarget
1689	{bust_invigilate_windows}::
1690	`buw-jpIW` —
1691	{busn_invigilate} ceremony on a Windows OpenSSH node: read-only
1692	posture verification.
1693

1695	//axvo_tabtarget
1696	{bust_invigilate_macos}::
1697	`buw-jpIM` —
1698	{busn_invigilate} ceremony on a macOS node: read-only posture
1699	verification.
1700	
1701	[[bust_invigilate_linux]]
1702	//axvo_tabtarget
1703	{bust_invigilate_linux}::
1704	`buw-jpIL` —
1705	{busn_invigilate} ceremony on a Linux node: read-only posture

1707	
1708	==== Privileged SSH
1709	
1710	[[bust_privileged_ssh]]

1712	{bust_privileged_ssh}::
1713	`buw-jpS` —
1714	Thin admin-side pass-through that runs an arbitrary command on a

include::BUSD-DispatchRuntime.adoc[]

== Remote Node Access

BUK reaches remote nodes under the
{bus_jurisdiction}
umbrella: a single station user establishes admin SSH trust on the
node, provisions one workload account, and dispatches commands to it.

[[bus_jurisdiction]]
{bus_jurisdiction}::
The BUK feature-area for reaching remote nodes deterministically.
Three verbs cover the privileged-side ceremonies:
{busn_caparison}
establishes admin host posture per platform (SSH key trust,
`sshd_config` hardening on Windows OpenSSH, host-availability
state); {busn_invigilate} verifies that posture read-only as a
precondition for {busn_garrison} and as a post-completion check for
{busn_caparison}; {busn_garrison} provisions and refreshes a workload
account from an existing admin SSH foothold. The workload-side
tabtargets
({bust_knock}, {bust_command_file}, {bust_interactive_session})
dispatch work to that account.
+
{bus_jurisdiction}
is logical only — there is no AXLA-typed entity behind the umbrella.
Its implementation seat is
{bujb_jurisdiction}.

[[bujb_jurisdiction]]
{bujb_jurisdiction}::
The BCG-compliant module
(`Tools/buk/bujb_jurisdiction.sh` plus its `bujb_cli.sh` companion)
that houses
{busn_caparison},
{busn_invigilate},
and
{busn_garrison}
implementations and hardcodes the
{bus_jurisdiction}
contracts not expressed in regime data:
the three shell-letter→`command=` directive mappings
(`b` = native bash for Linux/Mac;
`c` = Cygwin;
`w` = WSL)
used by garrison on the workload account,
the workload private-key destination paths on the remote per shell-letter,
the canonical WSL distribution name (`rbtww-main`),
and the Windows OpenSSH `sshd_config` hardening directive set used by
caparison-windows.
+
SSH transport mechanics live in code;
{burp_regime}
carries no shell selection.

[[bujb_workload_user]]
//axvr_constant axf_bash
{bujb_workload_user}::
Project-wide constant name for the unprivileged workload OS user that
{busn_garrison}
provisions on every node — single source of truth, one workload user
per node, never operator-tunable.
Defined as a tinder constant in
{bujb_jurisdiction}
so it resolves at module-source time and is available to every
jurisdiction-domain function without enrollment.
On Windows nodes the same name covers both namespaces of the mirrored
identity (the Windows local user that
{busn_garrison} (`w`) creates via `net.exe user /add` and the Linux
user inside `rbtww-main` it creates via `useradd`).

=== Privileges

A
{burn_regime}
instance lives under `.buk/rbmn_nodes/<{burn_investiture}>/` and is shared
across station users (git-transported); it carries node shape only and
no key material.
A
{burp_regime}
instance lives under `.buk/rbmu_users/<{burs_user}>/<{burp_investiture}>/`;
the
{burs_user}
owns its credential subtree exclusively, and operations write only
under the current station user's tree.

{busn_garrison}
authenticates as
{burp_privileged_user}
using
{burp_privileged_key_file}
by key alone; admin trust and host posture are established
beforehand by
{busn_caparison}
per platform (Windows uses a one-time password fallback on its own
phase-1 admin SSH session for first-run trust; Linux/Mac wrap
ssh-copy-id orchestration).
Workload account is unprivileged, ssh-only, and named by
{bujb_workload_user}
(project-wide convention, single source of truth).

=== Garrison-Destructive Model

Every
{busn_garrison}
invocation wipes prior workload state on the node regardless of which
{burp_investiture}
or station user runs it.
There is exactly one workload user per node, ever; node runtime state
belongs to whoever last garrisoned it.
Garrison subsumes the workload-account lifecycle: there is no separate
verb to materialize, remove, or enumerate workload accounts — every
garrison rebuilds from a clean slate.
+
Admin-side host posture is out of garrison's scope.
{busn_caparison}
owns it per platform: Windows OpenSSH `sshd_config` hardening, WSL
distribution staging, sleep/hibernate disable, and Tailscale service
auto-start on Windows; Remote Login + pmset + Tailscale on macOS;
ssh-copy-id orchestration + sleep target masking + tailscaled on
Linux.
{busn_invigilate}
verifies the posture as garrison's precondition.

=== Tabtarget Catalog

Twenty-three tabtargets cover regime configuration, caparison,
invigilate, privileged SSH, garrison ceremony, workload dispatch,
and per-platform handbooks.
PowerShell garrison and dispatch are deferred and not enumerated below.

==== Regime Configuration

[[bust_node_regime_list]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_node_regime_list}::
`buw-rnl` —
List all {burn_regime} profiles under `.buk/rbmn_nodes/`.

[[bust_node_regime_render]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_node_regime_render}::
`buw-rnr` —
Render a single {burn_regime} profile
({burn_investiture} passed as positional argument).

[[bust_node_regime_validate]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_node_regime_validate}::
`buw-rnv` —
Validate a single {burn_regime} profile
({burn_investiture} passed as positional argument).

[[bust_privilege_regime_list]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_privilege_regime_list}::
`buw-rpl` —
List all {burp_regime} profiles for the current {burs_user}.

[[bust_privilege_regime_render]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_privilege_regime_render}::
`buw-rpr` —
Render a single {burp_regime} profile
({burp_investiture} passed as positional argument).

[[bust_privilege_regime_validate]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_privilege_regime_validate}::
`buw-rpv` —
Validate a single {burp_regime} profile
({burp_investiture} passed as positional argument).

==== Caparison (per platform)

[[bust_caparison_windows]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_caparison_windows}::
`buw-jpCW` —
{busn_caparison} ceremony on a Windows OpenSSH node: SSH-trust
hardening, WSL distribution staging, powercfg sleep/hibernate
disable, and Tailscale service auto-start.
Capital `C` marks the verb (Caparison); capital `W` marks the
platform (Windows).

[[bust_caparison_macos]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_caparison_macos}::
`buw-jpCM` —
{busn_caparison} ceremony on a macOS node: Remote Login enable,
ssh-copy-id orchestration, pmset power configuration, Tailscale
service auto-start.

[[bust_caparison_linux]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_caparison_linux}::
`buw-jpCL` —
{busn_caparison} ceremony on a Linux node: ssh-copy-id orchestration,
systemctl sleep target masking, tailscaled enable.

==== Invigilate (per platform)

[[bust_invigilate_windows]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_invigilate_windows}::
`buw-jpIW` —
{busn_invigilate} ceremony on a Windows OpenSSH node: read-only
posture verification.

[[bust_invigilate_macos]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_invigilate_macos}::
`buw-jpIM` —
{busn_invigilate} ceremony on a macOS node: read-only posture
verification.

[[bust_invigilate_linux]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_invigilate_linux}::
`buw-jpIL` —
{busn_invigilate} ceremony on a Linux node: read-only posture
verification.

==== Privileged SSH

[[bust_privileged_ssh]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_privileged_ssh}::
`buw-jpS` —
Thin admin-side pass-through that runs an arbitrary command on a
node as {burp_privileged_user} under key-only auth.
Pass-through: argv after the {burp_investiture} is handed to ssh
as the remote command without shell wrapping; the operator
prepends `powershell -Command`, `bash -c`, or whatever the
target platform requires.

[[busn_caparison]]
//axl_voices axt_command
{busn_caparison}::
Establish admin host posture per platform — SSH key trust plus the
host-side state (sshd_config hardening on Windows OpenSSH, WSL
distribution staging, power/sleep configuration, Tailscale service
auto-start where applicable) — leaving the host ready for
{busn_garrison} and {busn_invigilate} verification.
Per-platform tabtargets
(`buw-jpCW`, `buw-jpCM`, `buw-jpCL`);
each implements its platform's posture establishment from a single
operator-facing verb.
Nuclear: every invocation purges and re-establishes the
caparison-managed posture from scratch — no skip-if-present logic.
Operator-managed scope (security-sensitive registry edits, Tailscale
interactive auth, etc.) is documented per-platform in
{bust_handbook_windows},
{bust_handbook_macos},
and
{bust_handbook_linux};
caparison cannot or does not automate that scope.
+
On Windows the SSH-trust phases retain a structural two-phase shape
forced by `Restart-Service sshd` terminating its own ssh session:
phase 1 (over the password-or-key admin SSH session) places the
admin pubkey, applies icacls lockdown, writes hardened sshd_config
directives, validates via `sshd -t`, and restarts sshd; phase 2
(key-auth-only reconnect) verifies the post-restart admin session
authenticates by key alone. Post-trust admin posture (WSL stage,
powercfg, Tailscale service) runs after phase 2. The bash
orchestrator treats the disconnect immediately after
`Restart-Service sshd` as the expected phase boundary, not a failure.

[[busn_invigilate]]
//axl_voices axt_command
{busn_invigilate}::
Verify admin host posture per platform, read-only — assert each
posture fact (admin SSH key trust, service states, registry values,
power configuration, host-availability resources) matches its
expected value, with no mutations and no remediation.
Per-platform tabtargets
(`buw-jpIW`, `buw-jpIM`, `buw-jpIL`);
each owns its platform's posture fact list.
Failure pointers discriminate operator-managed posture
(→ the per-platform handbook) from caparison-managed posture
(→ the per-platform caparison).
+
Used twice: as
{busn_caparison}'s post-completion check (caparison runs invigilate
after applying its operations to verify they took), and as
{busn_garrison}'s precondition check (garrison runs invigilate
before any destructive workload work).

[[busn_garrison]]
//axl_voices axt_command
{busn_garrison}::
Provision or refresh the workload account on a node from an existing
admin SSH foothold.
A single tabtarget per shell-letter
(`buw-jpGb`, `buw-jpGc`, `buw-jpGw`)
runs the same destructive ceremony with the shell-letter selecting
remote shell mappings via
{bujb_jurisdiction}.
Garrison never reads or writes any `sshd_config` and never alters
admin authorized_keys; first-step admin SSH must succeed by key auth
alone, surfacing any missing admin trust immediately.
Idempotent on re-run; destructive of any prior workload state.
+
Ceremony steps:
+
. SSH as
{burp_privileged_user}
using
{burp_privileged_key_file};
key-auth only (no password fallback). Across platforms, this
presupposes
{busn_caparison}
has run for this {burp_investiture};
{busn_invigilate}
runs as the precondition assertion before this SSH attempt to
surface posture failures with specific guidance.
. Obliterate any existing workload account named
{bujb_workload_user}
via `zbujb_obliterate_workload` — the letter-agnostic, platform-aware
destroy helper. Scope spans every namespace a prior garrison of any
letter could have written to (on Windows: SAM entry, Windows profile
directory, Cygwin home, WSL-side Linux user inside `rbtww-main`; on
Linux/Mac: native account + home). Idempotent on absent state.
. Create fresh workload account named
{bujb_workload_user}:
unprivileged, no sudo, no admin group, ssh-only.
. Place workload pubkey
(derived from
{burp_workload_key_file}
via `ssh-keygen -y`)
in workload's authorized_keys with the shell-letter's `command=`
directive.
. Copy
{burp_workload_key_file}
to the remote workload account at the path hardcoded in

input.content:
//axhob_operation
{bust_invigilate_windows}

NOTE: {busn_invigilate} ceremony on Windows — read-only host posture
verification. Asserts each posture fact (admin SSH key trust,
operator-managed registry keys, Tailscale service StartupType,
powercfg sleep/hibernate state, AoAc override, admin's `rbtww-main`
WSL distribution presence) matches its expected value. No mutations,
no remediation. Failure pointers discriminate operator-managed
posture (→ {bust_handbook_windows}) from caparison-managed posture
(→ {bust_caparison_windows}).

Invocation contexts: {bust_caparison_windows} runs invigilate-windows
as its post-completion check (a non-zero exit signals that a
caparison operation did not take or that the operator-managed posture
is not in place); {bust_garrison_cygwin} and {bust_garrison_wsl} run
it as their precondition assertion before any destructive workload
work, so a missing or stale admin posture surfaces with platform-aware
guidance rather than an opaque downstream failure.

Read commands are PowerShell-side (via the admin SSH session opened
with {burp_privileged_key_file}): `Get-ItemPropertyValue` for registry
facts, `Get-Service` for service state, `powercfg /a` for sleep
states, `wsl.exe --list --quiet` for WSL distribution registration.
Bash-side BCG-compliant matching extracts each fact and compares to
its expected value; PowerShell stays out of the parsing business.

//axhopt_typed_parameter
{burp_investiture}

//axhoq_precondition
A {burp_regime} profile exists at the given {burp_investiture} and its
referenced {burn_regime} profile targets a Windows OpenSSH node
reachable as {burp_privileged_user} under key-only authentication
using {burp_privileged_key_file}. Invigilate does not establish that
trust — it presupposes it; admin SSH failure surfaces as the first
posture fact.

//axhos_step
. *Open admin SSH*: connect as {burp_privileged_user} using
{burp_privileged_key_file}; key-auth only (no password fallback).
Failure here is the first posture fact and surfaces a missing or
stale admin trust — the diagnostic points to {bust_caparison_windows}
since admin trust is caparison-managed.

//axhos_step
. *Assert operator-managed registry keys*: read each
{bust_handbook_windows}-established registry value via
`Get-ItemPropertyValue` and compare to its expected value. The
canonical fact in this group is `DevicePasswordLessBuildVersion`
(under `HKLM\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\PasswordLess\Device`),
the registry key the netplwiz auto-login GUI requires before it will
expose the "Users must enter a user name and password" checkbox.
Mismatch surfaces a diagnostic naming the key, its expected value,
and pointing to {bust_handbook_windows} — these edits are
security-sensitive and operator-manual; caparison neither writes
nor repairs them.

//axhos_step
. *Assert AoAc override*: read
`HKLM\System\CurrentControlSet\Control\Power\PlatformAoAcOverride`
via `Get-ItemPropertyValue` and assert it equals `0`. Modern Standby
(AlwaysOnAlwaysConnected) systems silently demote `powercfg`
sleep/hibernate disable to a no-op until this override is in place;
the override is a registry write that requires elevation and is
established once by the operator per {bust_handbook_windows}.
Mismatch points to {bust_handbook_windows}.

//axhos_step
. *Assert Tailscale service registered*: read the Tailscale service
record via `Get-Service Tailscale`. Absence indicates Tailscale was
never installed; the diagnostic points to {bust_handbook_windows}
(install + Run-Unattended toggle + first auth are operator-manual).

//axhos_step
. *Assert Tailscale service StartupType*: read the Tailscale service
StartupType via `Get-Service Tailscale | Select-Object StartType`
and assert it equals `Automatic`. Mismatch points to
{bust_caparison_windows} — caparison sets the StartupType after
Tailscale is installed, so a non-Automatic value indicates caparison
either did not run or its `Set-Service` operation did not take.

//axhos_step
. *Assert powercfg sleep/hibernate disabled*: invoke `powercfg /a`
and assert the output reports neither standby nor hibernate as
available. Mismatch points to {bust_caparison_windows} — disabling
sleep and hibernate is caparison's phase-3 operation; a node still
advertising those states means caparison either did not run or its
`powercfg` calls were demoted to no-ops by a missing AoAc override
(in which case the prior step has already pointed the operator to
the handbook).

//axhos_step
. *Assert admin's rbtww-main WSL distribution registered*: invoke
`wsl.exe --list --quiet` over the admin SSH session and assert
`rbtww-main` (canonical name hardcoded as `BUJB_wsl_distribution`
in {bujb_jurisdiction}) appears in the output. Absence points to
{bust_caparison_windows} — caparison's phase-3 `wsl --import` from
the Ubuntu-24.04 seed stages this distribution under the admin user;
a missing registration means caparison either did not run or its
import step failed without surfacing.

//axhog_guarantee
On success every posture fact has been asserted to match its expected
value, the running admin SSH session was used only for read commands,
and no remote or local mutation occurred. The node is in the shape
{bust_caparison_windows} produces and {bust_garrison_cygwin} /
{bust_garrison_wsl} require.

//axhoc_completion
Exit 0 when every posture fact matches its expected value.
Non-zero on the first mismatch, with a diagnostic naming the failing
fact, its expected value, its observed value, and the pointer
({bust_handbook_windows} for operator-managed facts;
{bust_caparison_windows} for caparison-managed facts).

You are authoring the body of an AsciiDoc spec file for the Recipe Bottle / BUK project. The work is part of a Job Jockey pace (heat ₣A-, pace ₢A-AA7) replacing the inherited fenestrate body of BUSJCW with the broader caparison-windows scope.

**Current state:** File holds the inherited fenestrate body (SSH-trust hardening only, two phases) plus a leading NOTE that says "Pace-1 skeleton retains the prior fenestrate ...". Read the file first.

**Goal:** Replace contents with a three-phase caparison-windows ceremony: Phase 1 = SSH-trust hardening (former fenestrate scope, mechanics already correct), Phase 2 = key-auth reconnect verification, Phase 3 = post-trust admin posture (WSL distribution stage, powercfg sleep/hibernate disable, Tailscale service auto-start) closing with an invigilate-windows post-completion check.

**Required structure (AsciiDoc with prefix-discriminated comment annotations):**

- `//axhob_operation` — operation header (single line `{bust_caparison_windows}` after it)
- A NOTE: paragraph block explaining the ceremony shape, three phases, and the structural disconnect after `Restart-Service sshd`. Drop the existing "Pace-1 skeleton..." NOTE entirely.
- `//axhopt_typed_parameter` — `{burp_investiture}`
- `//axhoq_precondition` blocks (each followed by paragraph). Required preconditions:
  1. A `{burp_regime}` profile exists at the given `{burp_investiture}` and its referenced `{burn_regime}` profile targets a Windows OpenSSH node.
  2. `{bust_handbook_windows}` operator-manual scope is complete — OpenSSH Server installed and reachable, security-sensitive registry edits applied (DevicePasswordLessBuildVersion, PlatformAoAcOverride), Tailscale installed with Run-Unattended + first auth, netplwiz auto-login configured, and the admin user has a known password. Reference `{bust_handbook_windows}` rather than re-listing items inline.
  3. `{burp_privileged_user}` is a member of the Administrators group.
  4. `{burp_privileged_key_file}` is unencrypted and exists on the operator's station.
- `//axhos_step` blocks for the ceremony, in order:
  - Phase 1 (steps 1-7, mechanics same as the inherited fenestrate body but with the verb name renamed to "caparison" wherever the prose talks about the verb itself):
    1. Open admin SSH with `PreferredAuthentications=publickey,password` (TTY password fallback on first run; key auth thereafter).
    2. Place admin pubkey (derived via `ssh-keygen -y` from `{burp_privileged_key_file}`) in `administrators_authorized_keys` as a bare entry — no `command=` directive — performed before any sshd_config change so phase-2 reconnect can authenticate by key.
    3. icacls lockdown on `administrators_authorized_keys` (SYSTEM + Administrators only).
    4. Write hardened directives to `sshd_config`: PubkeyAuthentication yes, PasswordAuthentication no, PermitEmptyPasswords no. Idempotent.
    5. Verify written state via PowerShell `Get-Content` returning raw bytes; bash-side BCG-compliant matching extracts effective directive values.
    6. `sshd -t` validates the config (penultimate atomic op of phase 1).
    7. `Restart-Service sshd` — final atomic op of phase 1; ssh session terminates here, bash orchestrator treats the disconnect as the expected phase boundary, not a failure.
  - Phase 2 (steps 8-9):
    8. Reconnect as `{burp_privileged_user}` with `PreferredAuthentications=publickey` (no password fallback). Failure here surfaces a brick — operator's manual password no longer opens a session.
    9. Verify post-restart admin session authenticates by key alone and the running sshd serves the hardened configuration.
  - Phase 3 (steps 10-13) — over the phase-2 admin session:
    10. Stage `rbtww-main` WSL distribution under admin user via `wsl.exe --import` from an Ubuntu-24.04 seed. Always purge-and-reimport — no skip-if-present logic. Canonical distribution name is hardcoded as `BUJB_wsl_distribution` in `{bujb_jurisdiction}`.
    11. powercfg sleep/hibernate disable (idempotent): `powercfg /change standby-timeout-ac 0`, `powercfg /change standby-timeout-dc 0`, `powercfg /hibernate off`. The disable depends on the operator-managed PlatformAoAcOverride having been set per handbook — invigilate-windows asserts that override; failure here on a Modern Standby system without the override would surface as the powercfg fact in the post-completion invigilate.
    12. Set Tailscale service StartupType Automatic and start the service (idempotent): `Set-Service -Name Tailscale -StartupType Automatic`, `Start-Service -Name Tailscale`. Tailscale itself was installed by the operator per handbook; caparison only sets the service shape.
    13. Run `{bust_invigilate_windows}` for this `{burp_investiture}` as the post-completion check. Non-zero exit indicates a caparison operation did not take or an operator-managed posture fact is missing; the diagnostic from invigilate names the failing fact and points the operator at the right repair surface.
- `//axhog_guarantee` paragraph: on success the node has admin SSH hardened to key-only with the operator's admin pubkey installed under correct ACLs, sshd_config carries the three hardening directives, post-restart admin session has been verified to authenticate by key alone, the rbtww-main WSL distribution is registered under the admin user, powercfg sleep/hibernate are disabled, the Tailscale service is set to Automatic and running, and `{bust_invigilate_windows}` has confirmed every posture fact end-to-end.

input.prompt:
You are authoring the body of an AsciiDoc spec file for the Recipe Bottle / BUK project. This is part of a Job Jockey pace (heat ₣A-, pace ₢A-AA7) replacing a skeleton TODO with the operator-manual handbook content for Windows.

**Target file:** `/Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_alpha_recipemuster/Tools/buk/vov_veiled/BUSJHW-HandbookWindows.adoc`

**Current state:** Skeleton — header NOTE plus "TODO: subsequent pace fleshes the step list and prose content." Read the file first to confirm and to see the existing intro NOTE you must preserve and extend.

**Goal:** Replace the TODO with the operator-manual procedure for Windows admin posture — the items `{busn_caparison}` cannot or should not automate. The handbook is rendered to the operator's terminal; no remote contact, no profile mutation.

**Required structure:**

- `//axhob_operation` — already present, line 1: `{bust_handbook_windows}` on line 2. Keep as-is.
- Preserve and lightly extend the existing NOTE block (line 4-12 area) — it's correct prose; you may tighten or add a sentence pointing to `{bust_caparison_windows}` as the downstream consumer that fails when handbook items are missing. Do not retain the "TODO: subsequent pace..." line.
- `//axhos_step` blocks for the operator procedure. **No `//axhopt_typed_parameter`** — handbook tabtargets are zero-arg dispatchers. Required steps (each a paragraph describing a concrete operator action with exact commands or GUI paths):

  1. *Install OpenSSH Server*: enable the OpenSSH Server optional feature. Either GUI (Settings → Apps → Optional features → Add feature → "OpenSSH Server"), or PowerShell elevated: `Add-WindowsCapability -Online -Name OpenSSH.Server~~~~0.0.1.0`.

  2. *Set sshd to Automatic and start*: `Set-Service -Name sshd -StartupType Automatic`, then `Start-Service -Name sshd`. Verify service is running with `Get-Service sshd`.

  3. *Open the SSH port through Windows Firewall*: `New-NetFirewallRule -Name sshd -DisplayName 'OpenSSH Server (sshd)' -Enabled True -Direction Inbound -Protocol TCP -Action Allow -LocalPort 22` (or whatever non-default port the BURN profile carries). The OpenSSH install on recent Windows builds creates this rule automatically; this step is a verify-and-add fallback.

  4. *Enable temporary password authentication*: edit `C:\ProgramData\ssh\sshd_config` to set `PasswordAuthentication yes`, then `Restart-Service sshd`. This is intentionally temporary — `{bust_caparison_windows}` flips it back to `no` during phase 1. Caparison's first-run admin SSH session uses `PreferredAuthentications=publickey,password` and falls through to a TTY password prompt; this step makes that fall-through possible on a fresh node.

  5. *Ensure the admin user has a known password*: `{burp_privileged_user}` must be a member of Administrators with a password the operator knows; required for caparison's phase-1 password fallback (the operator types it once on /dev/tty; caparison itself never sees the password). Set or verify with `lusrmgr.msc` or `net.exe user`.

  6. *Apply security-sensitive registry edits*: each requires elevation. Use `reg add` from an elevated cmd or `New-ItemProperty` from elevated PowerShell.
     - `DevicePasswordLessBuildVersion` = 0 under `HKLM\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\PasswordLess\Device` — required for the netplwiz auto-login GUI to expose the "Users must enter a user name and password" checkbox on Windows 10/11. Without this key the netplwiz step has no checkbox to uncheck.
     - `PlatformAoAcOverride` = 0 under `HKLM\System\CurrentControlSet\Control\Power` — required on Modern Standby (AlwaysOnAlwaysConnected) systems so caparison's `powercfg` sleep/hibernate disable will actually take. Without the override, `powercfg /change standby-timeout-ac 0` is silently demoted to a no-op.
  Both keys are asserted by `{bust_invigilate_windows}` as operator-managed posture facts; mismatches there point back at this handbook.

  7. *Install Tailscale*: download the Tailscale for Windows MSI from tailscale.com and install it. The installer registers a Windows service named `Tailscale`.

  8. *Toggle Tailscale Run-Unattended*: open the Tailscale tray menu → Preferences → enable "Run unattended". This lets the service start at boot and stay up without an interactive desktop session — a release-1 requirement because the host runs without console login during automated tests. `{bust_caparison_windows}` sets the service `StartupType Automatic`, but the service can only deliver useful traffic when Run-Unattended is on.

  9. *Authenticate Tailscale to the operator's tailnet*: open the Tailscale tray menu → Sign in → complete the browser-based first-time auth. This binds the host to the operator's tailnet so the BURN profile's tailnet hostname (or `100.x.y.z`) becomes a stable transport address.

  10. *Configure netplwiz auto-login*: run `netplwiz` (Windows + R → "netplwiz" → Enter). Select the admin user. Uncheck "Users must enter a user name and password to use this computer" (visible only after the registry edit in step 6). Apply, then enter the admin password twice when prompted. The host now boots straight to a logged-in admin desktop, which is the substrate Tailscale Run-Unattended and OpenSSH server depend on for unattended operation.

- `//axhog_guarantee` paragraph: no remote contact occurred and no local file was written by the handbook itself. The operator has the procedure rendered; performing the steps establishes the operator-managed scope `{bust_caparison_windows}` and `{bust_invigilate_windows}` depend on.

- `//axhoc_completion` paragraph: exit 0 after the handbook content is printed. Non-zero only when the handbook content cannot be located or rendered.

**Style anchor:** Existing handbook entry top-level dispatcher at `/Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_alpha_recipemuster/Tools/buk/vov_veiled/BUSJH0-HandbookJurisdictionTop.adoc` shows the shape. The other per-platform handbook stubs (BUSJHM, BUSJHL) are still skeletons; do not consult them.

**Linked terms (already-defined quoin attributes — use as-is):** `{busn_caparison}`, `{bus_jurisdiction}`, `{burp_privileged_user}`, `{bust_caparison_windows}`, `{bust_invigilate_windows}`, `{bust_handbook_windows}`.

**Constraints:**
- Spec prose only; no bash code edits.
- Do not edit any other file.
- Do not edit BUS0.
- Use Write to overwrite the file (it currently has 14 lines; you'll need to Read it first to satisfy the harness).

Confirm at end with: "Done. Wrote BUSJHW."

  7. *Install Tailscale*: download the Tailscale for Windows MSI from tailscale.com and install it. The installer registers a Windows service named `Tailscale`.

  8. *Toggle Tailscale Run-Unattended*: open the Tailscale tray menu → Preferences → enable "Run unattended". This lets the service start at boot and stay up without an interactive desktop session — a release-1 requirement because the host runs without console login during automated tests. `{bust_caparison_windows}` sets the service `StartupType Automatic`, but the service can only deliver useful traffic when Run-Unattended is on.

  9. *Authenticate Tailscale to the operator's tailnet*: open the Tailscale tray menu → Sign in → complete the browser-based first-time auth. This binds the host to the operator's tailnet so the BURN profile's tailnet hostname (or `100.x.y.z`) becomes a stable transport address.

  10. *Configure netplwiz auto-login*: run `netplwiz` (Windows + R → "netplwiz" → Enter). Select the admin user. Uncheck "Users must enter a user name and password to use this computer" (visible only after the registry edit in step 6). Apply, then enter the admin password twice when prompted. The host now boots straight to a logged-in admin desktop, which is the substrate Tailscale Run-Unattended and OpenSSH server depend on for unattended operation.

- `//axhog_guarantee` paragraph: no remote contact occurred and no local file was written by the handbook itself. The operator has the procedure rendered; performing the steps establishes the operator-managed scope `{bust_caparison_windows}` and `{bust_invigilate_windows}` depend on.

- `//axhoc_completion` paragraph: exit 0 after the handbook content is printed. Non-zero only when the handbook content cannot be located or rendered.

**Style anchor:** Existing handbook entry top-level dispatcher at `/Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_alpha_recipemuster/Tools/buk/vov_veiled/BUSJH0-HandbookJurisdictionTop.adoc` shows the shape. The other per-platform handbook stubs (BUSJHM, BUSJHL) are still skeletons; do not consult them.

**Linked terms (already-defined quoin attributes — use as-is):** `{busn_caparison}`, `{bus_jurisdiction}`, `{burp_privileged_user}`, `{bust_caparison_windows}`, `{bust_invigilate_windows}`, `{bust_handbook_windows}`.

**Constraints:**
- Spec prose only; no bash code edits.
- Do not edit any other file.
- Do not edit BUS0.
- Use Write to overwrite the file (it currently has 14 lines; you'll need to Read it first to satisfy the harness).

Confirm at end with: "Done. Wrote BUSJHW."

input.prompt:
You are aligning two AsciiDoc spec files in the Recipe Bottle / BUK project. This is part of a Job Jockey pace (heat ₣A-, pace ₢A-AA7) — admin-side host posture is now owned by `{busn_caparison}` and verified by `{busn_invigilate}`, so the garrison specs need to delegate to invigilate-windows rather than carry their own posture preconditions.

**Files to edit:**
- `/Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_alpha_recipemuster/Tools/buk/vov_veiled/BUSJGW-GarrisonWsl.adoc`
- `/Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_alpha_recipemuster/Tools/buk/vov_veiled/BUSJGC-GarrisonCygwin.adoc`

**Read both files first** so you understand their full structure before editing.

**Edits to BUSJGW-GarrisonWsl.adoc:**

1. **Remove** the entire `//axhoq_precondition` block currently reading "The WSL distribution `rbtww-main` (hardcoded as `BUJB_wsl_distribution` in {bujb_jurisdiction}) is installed on the Windows host *under the admin user* and is the seed source for workload distributions. Admin keeps rbtww-main pristine per handbook discipline; garrison-w exports it to a seed tarball and the workload imports that tarball into its own HKCU. Garrison-w verifies admin's rbtww-main in a pre-flight after admin SSH succeeds; absence surfaces a diagnostic naming the constant and emitting copy-paste operator hints in the `tt/buw-jpS <investiture> '…'` shape rather than letting an opaque `wsl.exe` failure escape downstream." — that posture is now `{bust_caparison_windows}`'s scope and `{bust_invigilate_windows}`'s assertion.

2. **Add** a new `//axhoq_precondition` block immediately after the `{busn_caparison} has run for this {burp_investiture}...` precondition (or replacing the location of the deleted block — operator's choice) reading:

```
//axhoq_precondition
{bust_invigilate_windows} passes for this {burp_investiture}: admin
SSH key trust is in place, the operator-managed registry posture is
correct, the Tailscale service is registered and set to Automatic,
powercfg sleep/hibernate are disabled, and the `rbtww-main` WSL
distribution (canonical name hardcoded as `BUJB_wsl_distribution` in
{bujb_jurisdiction}) is registered under the admin user. Garrison
runs invigilate as its first action so a missing or stale admin
posture surfaces with a fact-named diagnostic rather than as an
opaque downstream failure.
```

3. **Tighten** the existing step currently titled "*Pre-flight + export seed*" so that it no longer claims to verify admin's rbtww-main (invigilate now handles that as a precondition). Replace the step body with prose along the lines of:

```
. *Export seed*: export admin's `rbtww-main` distribution to a
workload-readable seed tarball under a path inside the workload's
profile (admin can write there before the workload first logs in,
and the workload's session can read it). Caparison has staged
`rbtww-main` and invigilate has verified its presence as a
precondition; this step takes that presence as given. The tarball
is the sole on-disk artifact carrying admin's `rbtww-main` contents
into the workload's identity scope; garrison removes it on success.
```

Rename the step heading from "Pre-flight + export seed" to "Export seed" to match the body.

4. **Add an early step** for the invigilate precondition assertion. Insert a new `//axhos_step` block as the first step (before "Open admin SSH"):

```
//axhos_step
. *Assert admin posture*: invoke `{bust_invigilate_windows}` for this
{burp_investiture}. Failure surfaces a fact-named diagnostic
({bust_handbook_windows} pointer for operator-managed facts;
{bust_caparison_windows} pointer for caparison-managed facts) and
aborts garrison before any destructive workload work.
```

Update the completion clause so its failure-list mentions invigilate-windows assertion failure as the first item.

**Edits to BUSJGC-GarrisonCygwin.adoc:**

1. **Add** a new `//axhoq_precondition` block immediately after the `{busn_caparison} has run for this {burp_investiture}...` precondition reading:

```
//axhoq_precondition
{bust_invigilate_windows} passes for this {burp_investiture}: admin
SSH key trust is in place, operator-managed registry posture is
correct, Tailscale service is set to Automatic, and powercfg
sleep/hibernate are disabled. Garrison runs invigilate as its first
action so a missing or stale admin posture surfaces with a
fact-named diagnostic rather than as an opaque downstream failure.
```

(BUSJGC differs from BUSJGW in that the rbtww-main WSL fact is not relevant to a Cygwin garrison run — Cygwin sshd is the workload shell substrate, not WSL.)

2. **Add** a new `//axhos_step` as the first step (before "Open admin SSH"):

```
//axhos_step
. *Assert admin posture*: invoke `{bust_invigilate_windows}` for this
{burp_investiture}. Failure surfaces a fact-named diagnostic
({bust_handbook_windows} pointer for operator-managed facts;
{bust_caparison_windows} pointer for caparison-managed facts) and
aborts garrison before any destructive workload work.
```

3. Update the completion clause so its failure-list mentions invigilate-windows assertion failure as the first item.

4. **Keep** the "Cygwin sshd is installed on the node" wording where it currently sits (as part of the existing caparison precondition or as a sibling). Invigilate-windows does not assert Cygwin sshd; that posture remains a separate, operator-managed precondition naming the cygwin handbook surface inline since it's not in invigilate's fact list.

**Constraints:**
- Use Edit (not Write) for all changes — preserve all other content verbatim including step bodies, guarantees, completion clauses.
- Do not edit any other file.
- Do not edit BUS0.
- No bash code edits.

Confirm at end with: "Done. Edited BUSJGW and BUSJGC."

content.content:
1	//axhob_operation
2	{bust_caparison_windows}
3	
4	NOTE: {busn_caparison} ceremony on Windows — three phases. Phase 1
5	(over a password-or-key admin SSH session) places the admin pubkey in
6	`administrators_authorized_keys`, applies icacls lockdown, writes
7	hardened `sshd_config` directives, validates via `sshd -t`, and
8	restarts sshd. The structural disconnect after `Restart-Service sshd`
9	is forced: sshd terminates its own ssh session on restart; the bash
10	orchestrator treats it as the expected phase boundary, not a failure.
11	Phase 2 (key-auth-only reconnect) verifies the post-restart admin
12	session authenticates by key alone and the running sshd serves the
13	hardened configuration. Phase 3 (over the phase-2 admin session)
14	applies post-trust admin posture: stages the `rbtww-main` WSL
15	distribution under the admin user, disables sleep and hibernate via
16	`powercfg`, and sets the Tailscale service to Automatic and running.
17	Nuclear: every invocation purges and re-establishes the
18	caparison-managed posture from scratch — no skip-if-present logic.
19	Closing step runs {bust_invigilate_windows} as the post-completion
20	check.
21	
22	//axhopt_typed_parameter
23	{burp_investiture}
24	
25	//axhoq_precondition
26	A {burp_regime} profile exists at the given {burp_investiture} and its
27	referenced {burn_regime} profile targets a Windows OpenSSH node.
28	
29	//axhoq_precondition
30	{bust_handbook_windows} operator-manual scope is complete: OpenSSH
31	Server is installed and reachable, security-sensitive registry edits
32	have been applied (DevicePasswordLessBuildVersion,
33	PlatformAoAcOverride), Tailscale is installed with Run-Unattended
34	toggle set and first auth completed, netplwiz auto-login is
35	configured, and the admin user has a known password. Consult
36	{bust_handbook_windows} for the full list; caparison presupposes this
37	scope, neither writes nor repairs it.
38	
39	//axhoq_precondition
40	{burp_privileged_user} is a member of the Administrators group.
41	
42	//axhoq_precondition
43	{burp_privileged_key_file} is unencrypted and exists on the operator's
44	station.
45	
46	//axhos_step
47	. *Phase 1 — open admin SSH*: connect as {burp_privileged_user} with
48	`PreferredAuthentications=publickey,password`. Steady-state:
49	{burp_privileged_key_file} authenticates by key. First run: ssh
50	prompts on /dev/tty for the admin password; operator types it once.
51	
52	//axhos_step
53	. *Phase 1 — place admin trust*: install the admin pubkey (derived
54	from {burp_privileged_key_file} via `ssh-keygen -y`) in Windows
55	OpenSSH `administrators_authorized_keys` as a bare key entry — no
56	`command=` directive. Performed before any sshd_config change so that
57	key auth is available for phase 2's reconnect. Nuclear: the file is
58	replaced each invocation; no skip-if-present logic.
59	
60	//axhos_step
61	. *Phase 1 — lock down administrators_authorized_keys*: apply
62	`icacls` so only SYSTEM and the Administrators group hold ACL entries;
63	Windows OpenSSH refuses to read the file otherwise. Idempotent.
64	
65	//axhos_step
66	. *Phase 1 — write hardened directives*: set `PubkeyAuthentication yes`,
67	`PasswordAuthentication no`, `PermitEmptyPasswords no` in
68	`sshd_config`. Idempotent — already-hardened configs converge to the
69	same byte sequence on re-run.
70	
71	//axhos_step
72	. *Phase 1 — verify written state*: PowerShell `Get-Content` returns
73	the raw `sshd_config` bytes; bash-side BCG-compliant matching extracts
74	each effective directive value and asserts it matches the intended
75	hardened value. Keeps PowerShell out of the parsing business.
76	
77	//axhos_step
78	. *Phase 1 — validate sshd_config*: `sshd -t` runs as the penultimate
79	atomic op of phase 1. A malformed config aborts the caparison ceremony
80	before it could brick the running sshd.
81	
82	//axhos_step
83	. *Phase 1 — restart sshd*: `Restart-Service sshd` is the final atomic
84	op of phase 1. The bash orchestrator treats the disconnect immediately
85	after this exec as the expected phase boundary, not a failure.
86	
87	//axhos_step
88	. *Phase 2 — reconnect via key auth*: re-establish admin SSH as
89	{burp_privileged_user} with `PreferredAuthentications=publickey`
90	(no password fallback). Failure here surfaces a brick — the operator's
91	manual password no longer opens a session.
92	
93	//axhos_step
94	. *Phase 2 — verify post-state*: confirm the post-restart admin
95	session authenticates by key alone and the running sshd serves the
96	hardened configuration.
97	
98	//axhos_step
99	. *Phase 3 — stage WSL distribution*: import the `rbtww-main` WSL
100	distribution under the admin user via `wsl.exe --import` from an
101	Ubuntu-24.04 seed. Nuclear: always purge-and-reimport — no
102	skip-if-present logic. Canonical distribution name is hardcoded as
103	`BUJB_wsl_distribution` in {bujb_jurisdiction}.
104	
105	//axhos_step
106	. *Phase 3 — disable sleep and hibernate*: invoke `powercfg /change
107	standby-timeout-ac 0`, `powercfg /change standby-timeout-dc 0`, and
108	`powercfg /hibernate off`. Idempotent. Depends on the
109	operator-managed `PlatformAoAcOverride` having been set per
110	{bust_handbook_windows} — {bust_invigilate_windows} asserts that
111	override; on a Modern Standby system without it the powercfg calls
112	are silently demoted to a no-op, which the post-completion invigilate
113	surfaces.
114	
115	//axhos_step
116	. *Phase 3 — set Tailscale service Automatic and running*: invoke
117	`Set-Service -Name Tailscale -StartupType Automatic` followed by
118	`Start-Service -Name Tailscale`. Idempotent. Tailscale itself was
119	installed and authenticated by the operator per {bust_handbook_windows};
120	caparison only sets the service shape.
121	
122	//axhos_step
123	. *Post-completion check — run invigilate-windows*: invoke
124	{bust_invigilate_windows} for this {burp_investiture}. Non-zero exit
125	indicates a caparison operation did not take or an operator-managed
126	posture fact is missing; the diagnostic from {bust_invigilate_windows}
127	names the failing fact and points the operator at the right repair
128	surface.
129	
130	//axhog_guarantee
131	On success the node has admin SSH hardened to key-only with the
132	operator's admin pubkey installed in `administrators_authorized_keys`
133	under correct ACLs, `sshd_config` carries the three hardening
134	directives, the post-restart admin session has been verified to
135	authenticate by key alone, the `rbtww-main` WSL distribution is
136	registered under the admin user, powercfg sleep and hibernate are
137	disabled, the Tailscale service is set to Automatic and running, and
138	{bust_invigilate_windows} has confirmed every posture fact end-to-end.
139	
140	//axhoc_completion
141	Exit 0 when the post-completion {bust_invigilate_windows} passes.
142	Non-zero on phase-1 admin SSH failure, pubkey-write failure, ACL
143	lockdown failure, sshd_config write failure, parsed-state mismatch,
144	`sshd -t` failure, phase-2 key-auth failure, `wsl --import` failure,
145	powercfg failure, Tailscale `Set-Service` or `Start-Service` failure,
146	or post-completion invigilate failure.
147

file.content:
//axhob_operation
{bust_caparison_windows}

NOTE: {busn_caparison} ceremony on Windows — three phases. Phase 1
(over a password-or-key admin SSH session) places the admin pubkey in
`administrators_authorized_keys`, applies icacls lockdown, writes
hardened `sshd_config` directives, validates via `sshd -t`, and
restarts sshd. The structural disconnect after `Restart-Service sshd`
is forced: sshd terminates its own ssh session on restart; the bash
orchestrator treats it as the expected phase boundary, not a failure.
Phase 2 (key-auth-only reconnect) verifies the post-restart admin
session authenticates by key alone and the running sshd serves the
hardened configuration. Phase 3 (over the phase-2 admin session)
applies post-trust admin posture: stages the `rbtww-main` WSL
distribution under the admin user, disables sleep and hibernate via
`powercfg`, and sets the Tailscale service to Automatic and running.
Nuclear: every invocation purges and re-establishes the
caparison-managed posture from scratch — no skip-if-present logic.
Closing step runs {bust_invigilate_windows} as the post-completion
check.

//axhopt_typed_parameter
{burp_investiture}

//axhoq_precondition
A {burp_regime} profile exists at the given {burp_investiture} and its
referenced {burn_regime} profile targets a Windows OpenSSH node.

//axhoq_precondition
{bust_handbook_windows} operator-manual scope is complete: OpenSSH
Server is installed and reachable, security-sensitive registry edits
have been applied (DevicePasswordLessBuildVersion,
PlatformAoAcOverride), Tailscale is installed with Run-Unattended
toggle set and first auth completed, netplwiz auto-login is
configured, and the admin user has a known password. Consult
{bust_handbook_windows} for the full list; caparison presupposes this
scope, neither writes nor repairs it.

//axhoq_precondition
{burp_privileged_user} is a member of the Administrators group.

//axhoq_precondition
{burp_privileged_key_file} is unencrypted and exists on the operator's
station.

//axhos_step
. *Phase 1 — open admin SSH*: connect as {burp_privileged_user} with
`PreferredAuthentications=publickey,password`. Steady-state:
{burp_privileged_key_file} authenticates by key. First run: ssh
prompts on /dev/tty for the admin password; operator types it once.

//axhos_step
. *Phase 1 — place admin trust*: install the admin pubkey (derived
from {burp_privileged_key_file} via `ssh-keygen -y`) in Windows
OpenSSH `administrators_authorized_keys` as a bare key entry — no
`command=` directive. Performed before any sshd_config change so that
key auth is available for phase 2's reconnect. Nuclear: the file is
replaced each invocation; no skip-if-present logic.

//axhos_step
. *Phase 1 — lock down administrators_authorized_keys*: apply
`icacls` so only SYSTEM and the Administrators group hold ACL entries;
Windows OpenSSH refuses to read the file otherwise. Idempotent.

//axhos_step
. *Phase 1 — write hardened directives*: set `PubkeyAuthentication yes`,
`PasswordAuthentication no`, `PermitEmptyPasswords no` in
`sshd_config`. Idempotent — already-hardened configs converge to the
same byte sequence on re-run.

//axhos_step
. *Phase 1 — verify written state*: PowerShell `Get-Content` returns
the raw `sshd_config` bytes; bash-side BCG-compliant matching extracts
each effective directive value and asserts it matches the intended
hardened value. Keeps PowerShell out of the parsing business.

//axhos_step
. *Phase 1 — validate sshd_config*: `sshd -t` runs as the penultimate
atomic op of phase 1. A malformed config aborts the caparison ceremony
before it could brick the running sshd.

//axhos_step
. *Phase 1 — restart sshd*: `Restart-Service sshd` is the final atomic
op of phase 1. The bash orchestrator treats the disconnect immediately
after this exec as the expected phase boundary, not a failure.

//axhos_step
. *Phase 2 — reconnect via key auth*: re-establish admin SSH as
{burp_privileged_user} with `PreferredAuthentications=publickey`
(no password fallback). Failure here surfaces a brick — the operator's
manual password no longer opens a session.

//axhos_step
. *Phase 2 — verify post-state*: confirm the post-restart admin
session authenticates by key alone and the running sshd serves the
hardened configuration.

//axhos_step
. *Phase 3 — stage WSL distribution*: import the `rbtww-main` WSL
distribution under the admin user via `wsl.exe --import` from an
Ubuntu-24.04 seed. Nuclear: always purge-and-reimport — no
skip-if-present logic. Canonical distribution name is hardcoded as
`BUJB_wsl_distribution` in {bujb_jurisdiction}.

//axhos_step
. *Phase 3 — disable sleep and hibernate*: invoke `powercfg /change
standby-timeout-ac 0`, `powercfg /change standby-timeout-dc 0`, and
`powercfg /hibernate off`. Idempotent. Depends on the
operator-managed `PlatformAoAcOverride` having been set per
{bust_handbook_windows} — {bust_invigilate_windows} asserts that
override; on a Modern Standby system without it the powercfg calls
are silently demoted to a no-op, which the post-completion invigilate
surfaces.

//axhos_step
. *Phase 3 — set Tailscale service Automatic and running*: invoke
`Set-Service -Name Tailscale -StartupType Automatic` followed by
`Start-Service -Name Tailscale`. Idempotent. Tailscale itself was
installed and authenticated by the operator per {bust_handbook_windows};
caparison only sets the service shape.

//axhos_step
. *Post-completion check — run invigilate-windows*: invoke
{bust_invigilate_windows} for this {burp_investiture}. Non-zero exit
indicates a caparison operation did not take or an operator-managed
posture fact is missing; the diagnostic from {bust_invigilate_windows}
names the failing fact and points the operator at the right repair
surface.

//axhog_guarantee
On success the node has admin SSH hardened to key-only with the
operator's admin pubkey installed in `administrators_authorized_keys`
under correct ACLs, `sshd_config` carries the three hardening
directives, the post-restart admin session has been verified to
authenticate by key alone, the `rbtww-main` WSL distribution is
registered under the admin user, powercfg sleep and hibernate are
disabled, the Tailscale service is set to Automatic and running, and
{bust_invigilate_windows} has confirmed every posture fact end-to-end.

//axhoc_completion
Exit 0 when the post-completion {bust_invigilate_windows} passes.
Non-zero on phase-1 admin SSH failure, pubkey-write failure, ACL
lockdown failure, sshd_config write failure, parsed-state mismatch,
`sshd -t` failure, phase-2 key-auth failure, `wsl --import` failure,
powercfg failure, Tailscale `Set-Service` or `Start-Service` failure,
or post-completion invigilate failure.

content.content:
1	//axhob_operation
2	{bust_handbook_windows}
3	
4	NOTE: Per-platform handbook surface for Windows — operator-manual scope
5	that {busn_caparison} cannot or should not automate. Renders to the
6	operator's terminal as the entry point into Windows-specific bootstrap
7	procedures: OpenSSH server install + reachability, security-sensitive
8	registry edits (UAC-required, e.g.,
9	`DevicePasswordLessBuildVersion`), Tailscale install + Run-Unattended
10	toggle + first auth, netplwiz auto-login, and any other Windows
11	posture an operator must establish before {bust_caparison_windows} can
12	run. No remote contact and no profile mutation. Steps missing from
13	this procedure will cause {bust_caparison_windows} to fail at the
14	point that depends on them.
15	
16	//axhos_step
17	. *Install OpenSSH Server*: enable the OpenSSH Server optional feature.
18	  GUI path: Settings → Apps → Optional features → Add a feature →
19	  search "OpenSSH Server" → Install.
20	  Elevated PowerShell equivalent:
21	  `Add-WindowsCapability -Online -Name OpenSSH.Server~~~~0.0.1.0`
22	  Verify installation with `Get-WindowsCapability -Online -Name OpenSSH.Server~~~~0.0.1.0`.
23	
24	//axhos_step
25	. *Set sshd to Automatic and start*: from an elevated PowerShell prompt,
26	  run `Set-Service -Name sshd -StartupType Automatic` then
27	  `Start-Service -Name sshd`.
28	  Verify the service is running with `Get-Service sshd` and confirm
29	  `Status` is `Running`.
30	
31	//axhos_step
32	. *Open the SSH port through Windows Firewall*: from an elevated
33	  PowerShell prompt, run:
34	  `New-NetFirewallRule -Name sshd -DisplayName 'OpenSSH Server (sshd)' -Enabled True -Direction Inbound -Protocol TCP -Action Allow -LocalPort 22`
35	  (substitute the non-default port if the BURN profile carries one).
36	  Recent Windows builds create this rule automatically during the
37	  OpenSSH install; this step is a verify-and-add fallback. Confirm
38	  the rule exists with `Get-NetFirewallRule -Name sshd`.
39	
40	//axhos_step
41	. *Enable temporary password authentication*: open
42	  `C:\ProgramData\ssh\sshd_config` in an editor running as
43	  Administrator. Set `PasswordAuthentication yes`, save, then restart
44	  the service with `Restart-Service sshd`. This setting is
45	  intentionally temporary — {bust_caparison_windows} flips it back to
46	  `no` during phase 1. Caparison's first-run admin SSH session uses
47	  `PreferredAuthentications=publickey,password` and falls through to a
48	  TTY password prompt; this step makes that fall-through possible on a
49	  fresh node before any key has been installed.
50	
51	//axhos_step
52	. *Ensure the admin user has a known password*: {burp_privileged_user}
53	  must be a member of the Administrators group and must have a password
54	  the operator knows. This credential is required for caparison's
55	  phase-1 password fallback — the operator types it once on `/dev/tty`;
56	  caparison itself never handles the password. Verify or set with
57	  `lusrmgr.msc` (GUI) or from an elevated cmd prompt:
58	  `net user <username> <newpassword>`.
59	
60	//axhos_step
61	. *Apply security-sensitive registry edits*: each entry requires
62	  elevation. Use `reg add` from an elevated cmd prompt or
63	  `New-ItemProperty` from an elevated PowerShell prompt.
64	+
65	`DevicePasswordLessBuildVersion` = `0` (DWORD) under
66	`HKLM\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\PasswordLess\Device`:
67	+
68	----
69	reg add "HKLM\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\PasswordLess\Device" /v DevicePasswordLessBuildVersion /t REG_DWORD /d 0 /f
70	----
71	+
72	Required for the netplwiz auto-login GUI to expose the "Users must
73	enter a user name and password" checkbox on Windows 10/11. Without
74	this key the netplwiz step has no checkbox to uncheck.
75	+
76	`PlatformAoAcOverride` = `0` (DWORD) under
77	`HKLM\System\CurrentControlSet\Control\Power`:
78	+
79	----
80	reg add "HKLM\System\CurrentControlSet\Control\Power" /v PlatformAoAcOverride /t REG_DWORD /d 0 /f
81	----
82	+
83	Required on Modern Standby (AlwaysOnAlwaysConnected) systems so
84	caparison's `powercfg` sleep/hibernate disable will actually take.
85	Without the override, `powercfg /change standby-timeout-ac 0` is
86	silently demoted to a no-op.
87	+
88	Both keys are asserted by {bust_invigilate_windows} as
89	operator-managed posture facts; mismatches there point back at this
90	handbook step.
91	
92	//axhos_step
93	. *Install Tailscale*: download the Tailscale for Windows MSI from
94	  https://tailscale.com/download and run the installer. The installer
95	  registers a Windows service named `Tailscale` and adds a system-tray
96	  icon. No configuration is needed at this stage.
97	
98	//axhos_step
99	. *Toggle Tailscale Run-Unattended*: open the Tailscale system-tray
100	  icon → Preferences → enable "Run unattended". This lets the Tailscale
101	  service start at boot and stay up without an interactive desktop
102	  session — required because the host runs without a console login
103	  during automated tests. Note: {bust_caparison_windows} sets the
104	  Tailscale service `StartupType` to `Automatic`, but the service can
105	  only deliver useful traffic once Run-Unattended is on.
106	
107	//axhos_step
108	. *Authenticate Tailscale to the operator's tailnet*: open the
109	  Tailscale system-tray icon → Sign in → complete the browser-based
110	  first-time authentication. This binds the host to the operator's
111	  tailnet so the BURN profile's tailnet hostname (or `100.x.y.z`
112	  address) becomes a stable transport address that persists across
113	  reboots.
114	
115	//axhos_step
116	. *Configure netplwiz auto-login*: press Windows + R, type `netplwiz`,
117	  press Enter. In the User Accounts dialog, select {burp_privileged_user}.
118	  Uncheck "Users must enter a user name and password to use this
119	  computer" (this checkbox is visible only after the
120	  `DevicePasswordLessBuildVersion` registry edit in the step above).
121	  Click Apply, then enter the admin password twice when prompted. The
122	  host now boots straight to a logged-in admin desktop, which is the
123	  substrate Tailscale Run-Unattended and the OpenSSH server depend on
124	  for unattended operation.
125	
126	//axhog_guarantee
127	No remote contact occurred and no local file was written by the
128	handbook itself. The operator has the procedure rendered; performing
129	the steps above establishes the operator-managed posture scope that
130	{bust_caparison_windows} and {bust_invigilate_windows} depend on.
131	
132	//axhoc_completion
133	Exit 0 after the handbook content is printed.
134	Non-zero only when the handbook content cannot be located or rendered.
135
